summary: Administrative accounting register from an Inca tambo (waystation) at Huaca Perez / Tambo Inca in the Chillon Valley near Lima. 70 cords, 8 colors, 0 STRING (purely numerical — ALBA syllabary does not apply). 54% empty = pre-formatted register never fully completed. Cluster 1 contains an ascending sequence (10->70) with R²=0.981 log-linear fit (growth factor 1.25/entry). Maximum value 70 appears 3 times = cap or standard unit. Two physical markers (M1, M2) divide the khipu. Part of the Huaca Perez trio: UR190 (417 cords), UR191 (28 cords), UR192 (70 cords). Musee Quai Branly, Paris.
interpretation:
IDENTIFICATION
  70 cords (L1: 59, L2: 11). Architecture: 0-SEUL.
  8 colors: AB(44), W(12), MB(6), KB(2), GG(2), ?(2), BY(1), BG(1).
  7 clusters (3 main data + 2 singletons + 2 separators).
  Provenance: Huaca Perez, Lima (a.k.a. Hacienda Infantas, Tambo Inca) — Chillon valley.
  Museum: Musee Quai Branly, Paris (cat. 71.1887.9.15). Incomplete.
  Collection trio: UR190 (417 cords), UR191 (28 cords), UR192 (70 cords) — all same site, acquired 1887.
  0 STRING (purely numerical), 38 INT, 32 EMPTY (54%).
  OKR: 'M1 and M2 are composed of threads wrapped around primary cord; very frayed.' Physical markers.

HYPOTHESIS
  UR192 is a small administrative accounting register from an Inca tambo recording counts for ~11-14 entities of increasing size — possibly tribute groups, livestock herds, or storage units.

CLUSTER 1 — ASCENDING SEQUENCE (14 L1 cords, sum=448)
  Values: 10, 0, 12, 12, 16, 19, 25, 37, 43, 53, 70, 36, 45, 70.
  C1-C11 follows log-linear curve: R²=0.981, growth factor 1.252 per step.
  This regularity is NOT random — deliberately structured ranking.
  C12-C14 break pattern (36, 45, 70) = different category or summary entries.

CLUSTER 2 — SINGLETON (val=70, MB)
  Single cord at maximum value = TOTAL, reference, or divider.

CLUSTER 3 — SPARSE (9 cords, sum=11, 5 empty bicolor cords)
  Pre-formatted positions never filled.

CLUSTER 5 — SECOND ASCENDING SEQUENCE (18 cords, sum=53)
  Values: 5, 7, 8, 9, 11, 13, then 12 empties = interrupted register.

CLUSTER 7 — THIRD BLOCK (15 cords, sum=120, 11 empties)
  Values: 12, 17, 51, 40, then empty.

KEY OBSERVATIONS
  54% empty = pre-formatted register interrupted mid-completion.
  Two ascending sequences: Cluster 1 (10->70) and Cluster 5 (5->13).
  Different scales: Cluster 1 peaks at 70, Cluster 5 at 13 = different categories or administrative levels.
  Maximum value 70 appears 3 times = cap or standard unit.
  Note: With 0 STRING cords, the ALBA syllabary cannot contribute to interpretation. All analysis is structural and numerical.
references: Sivan 2026, ALBA Project — Ascending sequence analysis, R²=0.981 log-linear fit
Huaca Perez / Tambo Inca — Chillon Valley, Lima
Collection trio: UR190, UR191, UR192 — all same site and museum
